commits.message: Fix scanline overlay on welcome page
commits.body:
The CRT lines crept where they shouldn't go,
Interlacing the welcome's Three.js show.
Scoped to a class, the scanlines now know:
Only the main site gets that retro glow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Close Issue 751: blog top level + deploy
commits.body:
Build it, ship it, watch it fly—
One script to rule the deploy sky.
Blog posts roam free at repo root,
And docs will follow the same route.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The blog escaped its nested cage,
Moved to the root — a wider stage.
Docker's context grew to see
Both website/ and blog/ — now free.
Next stop: docs on every page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The blog broke free from website's nest,
To live at root with all the rest.
Widen context, ignore the chrome,
And docs will follow blog posts home.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add Issue 751: blog posts to top level
commits.body:
The blog lived in website's embrace,
But docs need a wider build space.
Move posts to the root,
Widen Docker's pursuit—
Then docs can take blog's former place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Three patches stacked in tidy rows,
A record of each change that flows.
Should the branch be lost or swept away,
These patches let us rebuild the day.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A link once whispered "_blank" and was lost,
No window, no tab — a silent cost.
Issue 639's ghost returned from the dead,
Re-applied the fix, same-tab instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The fix we wrote in six-three-nine,
Got lost in a refactoring mine.
Now the patch must return—
Same lesson to learn:
Suppress, defer, navigate in line.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A link said "_blank" and vanished to air,
No tab, no window, no page anywhere.
Issue 639 once knew how to cope—
Time to re-apply that same-tab hope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The flash has fled, the layer behaves,
Born where it belongs, no misplaced waves.
Two experiments tried, one lesson learned:
Don't build where you can't see — wait your turn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The layer once leapt before it looked,
And flashed where it was not yet booked.
Now the socket just saves the ID,
The render pass builds it where it should be—
Born in place, no position mistook.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Why create the layer, then move it?
Said the render pass, "I know the spot—
Just hand me the context ID,
I'll build it where it ought to be,
And spare us all that misplaced dot."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The overlay flashed because it showed
Before it knew just where to go—
Now hide it first, then place it right,
One frame of patience, no more fright.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Removing update_ca_layer_frame() caused the overlay to flash at (0,0)
instead of the wrong pane — worse, not better. The flash is caused by
the timing gap between CALayerHost creation and the first paint_pass(),
not by having two positioning authorities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Two functions placed the overlay frame—
One right, one wrong, both staking their claim.
  Delete the pretender,
  Let paint_pass be the sender:
One source of truth ends the blame game.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A webview born in a split on the right,
First appears on the left — what a sight!
  Two paths set the frame,
  But one has no name
For pos.left, so it guesses. Not quite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Cmd+C tried to flee,
Through menus and multi-pass—
Now it's home. Ctrl+V.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The menu bar said "Cmd+C? That's mine!"
The key pipeline said "Get in line!"
  We carved out five keys,
  For the browser to seize,
Now copy and paste work just fine.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Physical keys and keybinding passes—
The multi-pass pipeline surpasses
  Our blanket bypass.
  Now a targeted class:
Five Cmd+keys get surgical passes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The multi-pass key pipeline laughed,
At our guard we had so deftly daft,
  "OnlyKeyBindings? Gone!"
  But three passes marched on—
And no keystroke reached the browser's craft.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix Issue 748: menu steals Cmd+C first
commits.body:
performKeyEquivalent said NO,
so the menu bar ran the show.
Now we catch c, v, x, a, z—
route through key_common instead,
and let try_forward_key decide where to go.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Simplify browse mode check in Issue 748
commits.body:
Two branches, same return—
dead code cleared in a single turn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Rewrite Issue 748 with correct root cause
commits.body:
The Mojo pipe was never to blame,
Nor Chromium's renderer playing a game.
raw_key_event steals Cmd+C for the terminal's sake,
While the browser sits idle—a simple mistake.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Cmd+C was sent but nothing came,
The Mojo pipe had no one to blame.
ClipboardHost unbound,
No pasteboard was found—
Now an issue exists to stake its claim.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The posts lay wrapped in HTML lace,
But agents craved a simpler place.
Now curl the .md, the source is bare—
Front matter, prose, it's all right there.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Move GitHub link outside commit card
commits.body:
The link lived inside the box,
Crowding the message like mismatched socks.
Now it sits underneath,
Muted, discreet, beneath—
Outside the └── it quietly talks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The formatter came through like a breeze,
Trimming whitespace and tightening trees.
Sixteen files aligned,
Every bracket refined—
Now the linter can rest, if you please.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Highlight protocol description on home page
commits.body:
One sentence glows bright,
Accent on background inverse—
Like grep found the match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Fresh data from the log,
Two hundred lines reshuffled—
The commits remember.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix ASCII art line spacing in blog posts
commits.body:
The art had gaps between each row,
Like a picket fence letting wind blow.
line-height: 1 —
Now the lines touch, it's done,
Box-drawing chars in one seamless flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
CSS border-left, you served us well,
But beside ASCII lines you looked like hell.
Now one <pre> holds the frame—
┌ │ └ — all the same,
No gaps, no seams, one monospace shell.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Proper Case nav, titles, and view-all links
commits.body:
The labels stood lowercase, meek,
Now Proper Case gives them mystique.
Two "view all" links appear,
Muted, quiet, but clear—
For the curious operator to seek.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add page titles to all routes
commits.body:
Every tab said "TermSurf" — nothing more,
No hint of the page you'd come for.
Now blog, docs, about,
Each title rings out,
Like nameplates on every front door.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add voice guidelines to website CLAUDE.md
commits.body:
The aesthetic had monospace and scan lines,
But the words had no rules, no guidelines.
Now we write like it's '93,
Dispatches from cyberspace, free—
No "leverage." No "seamless." Just punch lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add GitHub link to hero section
commits.body:
No download yet, no binary to ship,
But the source is there for the taking.
A bracket link whispers, "fork this,"
To every cypherpunk in the making.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Update hero copy with cypherpunk tone
commits.body:
Hack the web, not "control" it—
Unlimited power, keyboard-lit.
Chromium, WebKit, overlaid,
In your terminal, unafraid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add hero tagline to home page
commits.body:
Root access to the 'net—
A tagline the homepage now wears.
No more jumping to posts,
First we make our boasts,
Then the content beneath it declares.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add server entry for static file serving
commits.body:
The SSR handler knew how to render,
But static assets? "Not my agenda."
So server.ts now checks the disk
For CSS and JS — no more risk,
Then falls through to the Start contender.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Fix CSS not loading in production
commits.body:
The ?url import pointed nowhere,
Production assets lost in thin air.
A plain import instead—
The framework's well-read,
It injects the styles with great care.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The markdown wandered far from home,
In docs/blog it used to roam.
Now nestled where the website lives,
No copy hack the deploy forgives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The server was custom-built, bespoke,
A Bun SSR artisanal yoke.
TanStack Start took the wheel,
Now blog posts reveal
Their markdown from disk when invoked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add blog pages with markdown rendering
commits.body:
Remark parses, rehype highlights,
KaTeX waits for math-filled nights.
Blog layout, listing, post by slug—
Prose styled dark in the cypherpunk mug.
Feeds in the head, the reader delights.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
A guide for agents yet to come,
On routes and themes and how things run.
Monospace, brackets, scanline haze—
All documented for future days.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add blog with first post and build pipeline
commits.body:
A post was born in docs/blog today,
TOML front matter showed it the way.
A script reads the markdown, feeds the machine,
Three feeds pour out — JSON, Atom, RSS stream.
The blog has a voice. Let it have its say.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Separate home page from blog page
commits.body:
Home is home and blog is blog,
Each deserves its own back-log.
Slash takes you where the landing lives,
Slash-blog is where the writing gives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add site navigation and page routes
commits.body:
Four brackets in a row they stand,
>[blog] [commits] [docs] [about] — the band.
The root now wraps, the welcome stays free,
Each page a route in the routing tree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
The dots were keen but out of line,
They clashed with text — a bad design.
The scanlines stay, the dots depart,
Sometimes less is better art.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Monospace glyphs and scanline haze,
Box-drawing borders, a brutalist phase.
Dot grids behind, no corners round,
[+] to expand what commits have found.
The terminal aesthetic, finally crowned.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.body:
Codesign warned of unsealed contents,
set -e took that as repentance.
The script died mid-stride,
webtui left outside—
Now || true grants it safe entrance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
commits.author: Ryan X. Charles

commits.message: Add light mode with Tokyo Night Day
commits.body:
The night was dark, the code was too,
But users in the sun had no clue.
Now Day is default, Night is dark—
prefers-color-scheme lights the spark.
